ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Closing The Estate
Off Calendar Apr-06-2022 Continued To May-04-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person. An Order to Appear will be issued to Administrator David H. Matchett and Attorney Camille Milder or Attorney Heather Matsumoto Hoekstra to appear on May-04-2022 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why they failed to appear and why the inventory and appraisal, and a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report on status of administration have not been filed. An Order to Show Cause re Sanctions will also be issued for the same time and date for counsel to show why sanctions in the amount of $250.00 should not be imposed. Status on close of estate is also continued to that date.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Gomez. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Oct-07-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Camille Milder, Esq., counsel for and with David Matchett, Petitioner (appearing separately by telephone). Present but not appearing: Carol Snow, interested person (appearing with Mr. Machett by telephone).

The Court recites the purpose of today's hearing and at the Court's inquiry, Ms. Milder reports that no objections have been received to the Petition. The Court notes that separate Probate Code Sec. 10532 authority has been requested and included in a separate attachment to the proposed Order. However, that authority is included in the general powers encompassed in the Independent Administration of Estates Act and as such, need not be requested separately. Ms. Milder withdraws the request and the Court removes the attachment from the proposed Order.

At the Court's query, Ms. Milder confirms that the proposed Executor is bondable as purportedly confirmed by the surety representative, Todd Christensen. Ms. Milder advises the Court that the bond calculation neglected to include rents received of $14,000 and should be increased by that amount. The Court sets bond at $2,781,000.

After recital of pertinent facts, the Court admits the decedent's holographic Will dated March 29, 2020 into probate and grants the Petition appointing David Matchett the Executor of the Estate of Stephen Chapman Matchett, siging the Order for same in open court.

[Subsequent to hearing, the Probate Examiner sets hearing on the status of the close of the estate for April 6, 2022.]

Judge: Hon. Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Reporter: Not Reported; Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (D204)
